label: MNDAA: Chin Shwe Haw → Laukkai
notes: MNDAA Operation 1027 main thrust capturing Kokang capital Jan 2024

label: TNLA/MNDAA: Northern Shan → Lashio
notes: TNLA and MNDAA coordinated advance capturing Lashio Aug 2024

label: AA: Maungdaw → Sittwe Corridor
notes: AA advance from captured Maungdaw pressing toward Sittwe (Rakhine capital)

label: AA: Ann → Sittwe Pressure
notes: AA advance from captured Ann (W. Military Command HQ) toward Rakhine capital Sittwe

label: KIA: Surrounding Myitkyina
notes: KIA tightening encirclement of Kachin State capital from multiple directions

label: KNLA: Hpa-An → Myawaddy
notes: KNLA advance capturing Myawaddy Apr 2024 (briefly); strategic Thai border trade corridor

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Depayin
damage: Charity concert destroyed; mass civilian casualties
notes: SAC jet bombers struck civilian concert in Kanbalu Township

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Pazigyi
damage: Village gathering destroyed; UN called 'act of pure terror'
notes: SAC jets and helicopter gunship attack on inauguration ceremony

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Let Yet Kone (IDP Camp)
notes: SAC jets struck Kachin State IDP camp during displacement crisis

label: SAC Airstrike Campaign: Naypyidaw → Loikaw Area
notes: Repeated SAC airstrikes on Kayah State targeting Karenni resistance positions and civilian areas

label: AA: Rakhine Coast → Paletwa (Chin State)
notes: AA extending operations northward into Chin State; captured Paletwa controlling Kaladan river corridor

label: PDF: Sagaing → Magway Operations
notes: PDF guerrilla operations extending from Sagaing heartland into Magway region; disrupting SAC supply lines

label: SAC Ground Offensive: Naypyidaw → Sagaing
notes: SAC major ground operations in Sagaing Region deploying combined infantry, armor, and airpower

label: SAC Ground Ops: Naypyidaw → Kayah State
notes: SAC combined arms operations against Karenni resistance in Kayah State with airstrikes and artillery

label: TNLA: Lashio → Hsipaw Advance
notes: TNLA pressing SAC positions in Hsipaw and Kyaukme townships in Phase 2 of Op 1027

label: SAC Supply Route: Mandalay → Kalay (Contested)
notes: Key SAC logistics corridor from Mandalay to northwestern garrison at Kalay; repeatedly disrupted by PDF ambushes

label: AA: Ann → Taungup Capture
notes: AA captured Taungup after 42-day siege; key road junction connecting Rakhine to Bago Region

label: MNDAA/TNLA: Laukkai → Muse Trade Corridor
notes: Resistance forces threatening SAC control of Muse border crossing; major disruption to China trade revenue

label: SAC Village Burning Campaign — Sagaing
notes: Systematic village destruction campaign in Sagaing; 800+ villages burned by SAC as counter-insurgency tactic

label: CDF/CNF: Hakha → Kalay Resistance Corridor
notes: Chinland Defence Force and CNF coordination along Chin-Sagaing border; connection to India-border resistance

label: SAC Airstrikes: Naypyidaw → Ayardaw (Sagaing)
notes: Two SAC jets conducted multiple strikes on Ayardaw Township; part of a surge killing 27+ Sagaing civilians between March 20–22

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Myaung Township
notes: Single SAC jet dropped 4 bombs on Myaung Township; continued near-daily aerial campaign against Sagaing villages

label: AA Assault: Maungdaw Corridor → Shwe Min Gan Naval Base
notes: AA launched coordinated assault on Shwe Min Gan Naval Base at ~09:00; fighters advanced to within 2 km of Sittwe city center; simultaneous Ann/Ngape attacks

label: AA: Ann → Ngape Concurrent Offensive
notes: AA attacked remaining SAC positions in Ann and Ngape simultaneously with Sittwe naval assault; multi-axis Rakhine-Magway corridor pressure

label: BWM/NMD-PDF: Rocket Attack on Magway Air Base
notes: BWM Mission 53: 11 x 107mm rockets fired at 23:20; Magway Air Base is primary SAC airstrike hub for Upper Myanmar

label: AA: Ann Area → Padan Township (Magway) Operations
notes: AA expanding operations northward into Magway Region; attacked junta Nat Yay Kan hilltop base; reflects AA multi-front pressure beyond Rakhine

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Yaw Region (Maulay Junction)
notes: Two waves: 4 jets at 13:22 (6x500lb bombs + 3 rockets) and 2 jets at 14:43 (2x300lb bombs); no active resistance presence reported

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Butalin Township (Sagaing)
casualties: ~10 civilians killed including pregnant women and infants
notes: 3 SAC jets struck Ngapayin village 13:00–14:15 local time; 4 bombs + rockets; no resistance presence reported; part of ongoing Sagaing aerial campaign

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Myaing–Pauk Oil Fields (Magway)
notes: SAC jets struck PDF units that had surrounded junta-controlled oil fields between Myaing and Pauk townships; reflects junta air campaign to defend Magway petroleum assets from PDF encirclement

label: KIA: Myitkyina Area → Hpakant (6 SAC Positions Captured)
notes: KIA captured 6 SAC frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi in Hpakant Township; jade-mining heartland offensive threatening SAC revenue and encirclement of Myitkyina

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Shwebo IDP Camp (Sagaing)
damage: Displacement camp and school struck; monk teaching children killed
notes: 7 bombing runs on civilian displacement camp in Shwebo Township; targets civilians sheltering from prior SAC operations

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Minbu-Saku Area (Magway)
casualties: 3 civilians wounded; 10+ SAC troops injured in ground fighting
notes: Day 6 of sustained combat in Minbu-Saku; SAC air support for ground troops; school bombed; separate 5-run strike on Pauk Township same day

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Wuntho Township (N. Sagaing)
notes: SAC jets struck villages in Wuntho Township as part of near-daily Sagaing aerial campaign; exact casualties unverified; part of 100+ civilian killing spree since junta parliament convened Mar 16

label: Joint Resistance: Ngape Township Kinpwonchaung Gate Raid
notes: PRA-Magway, CDF-Asho, New Society Army, Minbu Bn 8, and Ngape PDT raided Kinpwonchaung gate at 05:00; 2.5-hour battle; seized MA-1 rifle, 2 carbines, ammunition

label: LLSBF: Drone Strike on Launglon Police Station (Tanintharyi)
notes: Launglone Steel Butterfly Force dropped 2 drone bombs at 22:00 on junta personnel gathered at police station; southern Tanintharyi resistance expanding drone operations

label: KIA/KPDF: Drone Bombs on Junta Hilltop — Hpakant
notes: KIA and KPDF dropped drone bombs on strategic junta hilltop position at ~12:00; struck trenches; junta retaliated with airstrikes on Hmawsi Khtaung ward within 30 min; intense fighting at Prayer Hill and Cemetery Hill throughout April 11

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Hpakant Civilian Areas (Kachin)
damage: Hmawsi Khtaung ward, garbage dump village area, Cemetery Hill bombed
notes: SAC retaliatory airstrike within 30 min of KIA/KPDF drone attack; collective punishment targeting civilian Hmawsi Khtaung ward and Prayer Hill areas; sustained artillery fire throughout day

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Seik Hkun Monastery (W. Shwebo, Sagaing)
casualties: 2 monks killed; 8 novice monks + 3 civilians injured
damage: Monastery dormitories, dining halls, wells, and school building destroyed
notes: Thingyan eve airstrike on Buddhist monastery west of Shwebo; school and nearby homes also struck; second strike hit Tan Zin and Ponnagyun villages same day

label: SAC Jets: Naypyidaw → Hpakant Thingyan Continuation (Kachin)
notes: Two SAC jets dropped ~5 bombs on Ma Shi Ka Htaung near Sutaunggon Mountain at ~13:00; separate drone strikes at ~15:00 and later; ~100 SAC troops deployed ~16:00; third day of sustained Hpakant escalation

label: SAC Gyrocopter Attacks: NW Regional Cmd → Salingyi/Yinmabin (Sagaing)
notes: SAC Northwestern Regional Military Command gyrocopter attacks on Weikwe village (Salingyi Twp) and Leingnaut village (Yinmabin Twp) on Thingyan eve; infantry gunfire from low-altitude aircraft

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Pauk Township (Magway) — Thingyan Day
notes: SAC airstrikes on Pauk Township (Magway) and Luthaw Township on Myanmar New Year's Day; 6 killed during Thingyan celebrations; SAC maintained full military operations throughout the New Year holiday

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Mutraw/Dae Bu Noh (Karen State)
damage: Dae Bu Noh junior college building destroyed; village area struck
notes: SAC jets struck village gathering in Mutraw (Papun) District, KNU-administered Kawthoolei, on Thingyan Apr 15; school destroyed — had already relocated twice from prior attacks

label: SAC Airstrike: Naypyidaw → Mrauk-U / Ponnagyun (Rakhine State)
notes: SAC jets dropped 500-pound bombs on Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun (Rakhine); junta uses airpower against AA-controlled territory it can no longer hold on ground

label: KIA: Landmine Ambush on Militia Column — Hpakant
notes: KIA landmine ambush at ~08:00 on pro-junta militia column in Hpakant Twp; among highest single-action militia casualties in Kachin 2026; part of sustained KIA offensive targeting SAC revenue from jade mining

label: SAC Jets: Naypyidaw → Bue So KNU Hospital (Karen/Thai Border)
casualties: 6 AA members killed; additional KNU casualties unclear
damage: KNU hospital and civilian areas struck; 1 bomb crossed into Thailand (Mae Wan area, Mae Hong Son)
notes: Yak-130 and MiG-29 jets dropped 9 bombs on KNU hospital ~500m from Salween River/Thai border; 1 bomb landed in Mae Hong Son, Thailand; Thai 36th Ranger Forces Regiment responded; cross-border strike threatens Thailand-Myanmar diplomatic relations

label: SAC Jets: Naypyidaw → Taze Township (Sagaing)
casualties: 1 civilian injured; motorcycle shop and 5 vehicles destroyed
notes: 3 SAC jets from Tada-U and Meiktila air bases conducted 8 strikes on Kantuu Ma and Inyay Oe villages; civilian infrastructure targeted; post-Thingyan continuation of Sagaing aerial campaign

label: KIA: Hpakant → Karmaing Road Closure (Indefinite)
notes: KIA announced indefinite closure of Hpakant–Nantyar–Karmaing road on Apr 23; cuts SAC resupply to Hpakant garrison and degrades junta jade revenue; follows KIA captures of 6 SAC positions (Apr 2), drone attack (Apr 11), and ~40 militia killed in ambush (Apr 19)
